name: No-Knead Cheddar Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 package Active Dry Yeast; OR 1 tbsp Active Dry Yeast; Bulk
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the grated cheese in a small bowl and cover to prevent drying. Set aside.
2. Combine 1 1/2 cups unsifted flour, yeast, sugar, and salt in a large mixer bowl, blending thoroughly.
3. Measure 3/4 cup milk, water, and butter into a saucepan and heat until the liquids are warm (115 to 120 degrees F).
4. Gradually add the liquids to the dry ingredients in the mixer bowl, beating for 2 minutes at medium speed of the electric mixer, scraping the bowl occasionally.
5. Add and beat in 1 cup unsifted flour at high speed. Beat for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
6. Mix in enough additional flour (1/2 to 1 cup unsifted) to make a soft dough. The dough will be slightly sticky.
7. Place the dough into a greased deep bowl. Cover with waxed paper and a clean towel and let stand in a warm place until the dough has doubled, about 45 to 60 minutes.
8. Generously grease several baking sheets.
9. Melt the butter and set aside.
10. Punch the dough down using your fist and turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface.
11. Divide the dough into two equal portions. Set one portion aside.
12. Roll the dough into a rectangle 16 x 8-inches.
13. Brush with about half of the melted butter.
14. Sprinkle with about half of the grated cheddar cheese.
15. Cut crosswise into 8 equal portions.
16. Cut each portion into halves lengthwise, creating strips.
17. Fold each strip into thirds, lapping each side portion over the center third.
18. Place the rolls on a baking sheet.
19. Repeat for the other half of the dough.
20. Beat the egg yolk with the tablespoon milk, slightly. Brush the tops of the rolls with the egg yolk mixture.
21. Let rise until doubled, about 30 minutes.
22. Bake at 425 degrees F for about 8 minutes or until rolls are golden brown. Serve rolls hot.
